message:
fix(sync): refuse --rebuild's auto-recovery shortcut when it would drop user-requested import flags

`br sync --import-only --rebuild`, when the database file was missing
or invalid at open time, used to delegate the rebuild to
`OpenStorageResult::recover_database_from_jsonl` (the same path the
CLI uses for transparent recovery) and then short-circuit out of the
import stage. That shortcut is safe when the user only passed
`--rebuild` or `--force`, because those flags just ask for "the DB
should reflect the JSONL" — which recovery already did.

It is NOT safe when the user also passed `--rename-prefix`, because the
recovery path hard-codes `rename_on_import = false`. So the delegation
silently ignored the caller's request to rewrite imported IDs into the
configured prefix, and reported success.

This commit makes that combination explicit and actionable:

src/cli/commands/sync.rs
------------------------
- Adds `delegation_would_drop_user_flags` gating around the pre-import
  recovery delegation so the shortcut is skipped when `--rename-prefix`
  is set. The delegation still runs for plain `--rebuild`/`--force`.
- Adds `auto_rebuild_semantic_flag_conflict_reason()` which runs after
  `recover_database_from_jsonl` has already rebuilt, detects that the
  caller asked for prefix-rename semantics that the rebuild could not
  apply, and returns a VALIDATION_FAILED diagnostic that includes a
  ready-to-paste re-run command. The re-run command round-trips every
  relevant CLI override (`--db`, `--json`, `--quiet`, `--no-color`,
  `--actor`, `--allow-stale`, `--no-daemon`, `--no-auto-import`,
  `--no-auto-flush`, `--lock-timeout`, plus the original sync args)
  via a small `shell_quote` helper, so the operator can trust the
  printed command literally.
- Extracts `push_cli_rerun_overrides()` and `auto_rebuild_semantic_
  conflict_field()` to keep the conflict reporter readable.
- Adds `jsonl_contains_prefix_mismatch()` so a follow-up caller can
  tell whether --rename-prefix would actually do any work (skips
  tombstones).
- Extracts `emit_auto_rebuild_import_result()` — the human/rich/JSON
  render path for the "already rebuilt, nothing more to import" case
  was inlined in `execute_import`; pulling it out makes the new
  conflict path testable.

Tombstone-skip note inside the import loop is preserved: counting rows
from storage after an auto-rebuild gives the correct created count
because tombstones are discarded by import.

src/config/mod.rs
-----------------
- `OpenStorageResult::recover_database_from_jsonl` now sets
  `auto_rebuilt = true` itself instead of relying on every caller to
  flip the flag after the call returns. sync.rs previously set it
  manually right after the call, which worked but meant any new
  caller would silently fail to short-circuit the downstream import
  path. The helper is the right place for the flag because the flag
  documents what the helper did.

src/storage/sqlite.rs
---------------------
- Narrows `checkpoint_full` visibility from `pub` to `pub(crate)`.
  Nothing outside the crate calls it; keeping it crate-private
  prevents external consumers from relying on an implementation
  detail that is only meaningful inside the storage module.

tests/e2e_errors.rs
-------------------
Adds 5 new end-to-end cases exercising the new conflict path:
  - auto-rebuild + --rename-prefix on a prefix-mismatched JSONL →
    VALIDATION_FAILED with the re-run command echoed in the message.
  - the re-run command path itself (copy-paste it back into br, verify
    it succeeds and renames).
  - --rebuild without --rename-prefix still short-circuits cleanly
    via the auto-rebuild shortcut.
  - --force alone still short-circuits (does not trip the new guard).
  - plain `--import-only` when the DB is already healthy continues to
    hit the regular import path.
